answer:
**Varna** (*varṇa*, "color" or "class") refers to the foundational four-fold social division of humanity in the Smṛti tradition, originating from the cosmic body of the creator (Puruṣa or Svayambhuva Manu): Brahmanas from the mouth (priests/teachers), Kshatriyas from the arms (rulers/warriors), Vaiśyas from the thighs (merchants/farmers), and Śūdras from the feet (servants/laborers).[^1] These are assigned distinct, hierarchical duties (*svadharma*) to sustain cosmic order (*dharma*): Brahmanas teach/study Vedas, perform sacrifices, give/receive alms; Kshatriyas protect subjects, give/study/sacrifice; Vaiśyas tend cattle/trade/agriculture; Śūdras serve the twice-born without malice.[^2] Inter-*varṇa* unions produce mixed castes (*saṃkara*), ranked by *anuloma* (father higher) or *pratiloma* (father lower) descent, with the lowest (*caṇḍāla*, *pṛatiloma* offspring) dwelling outside villages.[^3] The Smṛti corpus (esp. *Manusmṛti*, primary source here; echoed in *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, *Kātyāyana Smṛti*) emphasizes rigid birth-based hierarchy, purity via *ācāra* (conduct), and pollution risks from mixing, but allows limited flexibility.

Movement between *varṇas* is exceptional, never upward casually, but permitted **downward** (*aparadharma* or *āpaddharma*, "distress dharma") when unable to subsist by one's own duties—temporary, reverting when possible.[^4] *Manusmṛti* Ch. 10 details:

- Brahmana unable to live by priestly duties adopts Kshatriya (arms/protection), then Vaiśya (trade/agriculture/cattle), avoiding farming (violent).[^5]
- Kshatriya/Vaiśya in distress adopt lower; Śūdra/artisan work.[^6]
- Examples: Viśvāmitra (Kshatriya origin) ate dog in famine; Bhāradvāja took cows from carpenter.[^7]
- Upward rare: 7th-generation elevation via austerity/seed (*retas*), or qualities (*guṇa*), but seed/field debated (seed superior).[^8]

Other Smṛtis affirm: *Kātyāyana* limits slavery/slave-work to same-*varṇa* (no Brahmin); *Viṣṇu* notes wealth from next-lower *varṇa* "speckled" (impure). No permanent/cross-generational fluidity emphasized; ideal is *svadharma*, even imperfect.[^9]

The corpus (37 works, ~Indic Smṛtis) lacks comprehensive "smriti corpus" genre-tag, but *Manusmṛti* dominates *varṇa*-discourse; others (*Nārada*, *Parāśara*) focus laws/penance without contradicting.

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 1, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-13a2db) (Mn_1.31); [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-71a5c2) (Mn_1.88-91).
[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 1, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-26a572).
[^3]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 10, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-4999b4) ff. (mixed castes, occupations).
[^4]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 1, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-96daa9) (Mn_1.116, previews *āpaddharma*); Ch. 10, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-233a88) (Mn_10.130).
[^5]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 10, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-b34355) (Mn_10.81-83).
[^6]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 10, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-53206d) (Mn_10.95-99).
[^7]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 10, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-f1a9ef) ff. (Mn_10.104-108).
[^8]: *Manusmṛti*, Ch. 10, [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-3faaee) (Mn_10.42); [translation/](works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/10/translation/#p-2cf995) (Mn_10.64-65).
[^9]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Ch. 63, [wiki card](works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/wiki/63.card.md); *Viṣṇu Smṛti* wiki notes "wealth acquired... next lower *varṇa* is speckled" (Ch. 58).
